description_long: This is a tall, narrow stone tower with a steep, dark, shingled roof and a rectangular base. The structure features two levels of arched windows, with the upper level having a pair of closely set windows and the lower level a single, smaller window. The stonework is dark and textured, giving the tower a robust, medieval appearance. The roof is slightly flared at the edges, adding a subtle decorative touch to the otherwise utilitarian design. At the base, there is a single arched doorway, suggesting an entrance for access. The overall form and details evoke the look of a fortified watchtower or gatehouse commonly found in historic European towns.
description: A tall, dark stone tower with a steep shingled roof, arched windows, and a single arched doorway at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched doorway at the base and two levels of arched windows above.
description_view_1: Side view with no visible entrance, showing the tower's narrow profile and window arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but with the arched doorway more clearly visible and no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with no entrance and the same window configuration.
